title: Building NairaWatch with an AI-Assisted Workflow
description: How a recurring frustration with fragmented FX data became a small, focused tool — and a practical case study in shipping with AI without losing the human judgment that matters.
content: NairaWatch didn't start as a grand product idea, but as something much simpler: discomfort. Working with international clients and moving between currencies, proposals, and invoices, I kept running into the same fragmentation — parallel market estimates here, official reference rates there, rumours in group chats, half-updated dashboards that didn't feel grounded in reality. Nothing felt coherent.

So I built NairaWatch — a small web app that tracks daily Naira estimates by combining parallel market signals with official reference rates across multiple currencies. It doesn't predict the future. It simply offers a clearer snapshot of where things are, so people working across borders can make slightly better decisions today.

The process was a practical test of an AI-assisted workflow I've been refining: clarify the idea and constraints, write a clear product context, rough out the visual direction, move into agent-style tools to expand and refine, then push to GitHub and deploy. AI in this process wasn't the hero — it was an assistant that helped me move faster by generating options, documenting edge cases, and surfacing scenarios I might miss. I still had to decide what to keep, change, and ignore.

I've always believed not every project needs to be a startup. Some things just need to exist as small, focused tools that serve a clear purpose for a specific group of people. NairaWatch is for anyone who lives in the tension between local realities and global systems — designers, developers, consultants, founders, anyone who has to think in more than one currency at a time.
